channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Oracle’s HR software now has AI to help with taxes and career planning
item.description: Oracle’s new AI will answer employee questions about everything job-related, from hiring to retiring. Oracle has embedded artificial intelligence capabilities into its Human Capital Management (HCM) software with the intention of optimizing workplace productivity, the company announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jocelyn Mintz, Fast Company

item.title: La falta de formación no merma la concepción estratégica de la IA
item.description: Con el punto y final de un 2024 marcado por la irrupción popular de la inteligencia artificial (IA), se podría decir que este nuevo año supondrá la consagración de la tecnología en el plano político, empresarial y social.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Google rolls out cheaper AI model as industry scrutinizes costs
item.description: Google has announced several updates to its Gemini portfolio, including a budget-friendly product, amid growing demand for low-cost AI models driven by the rise of Chinese competitor DeepSeek.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Get ready for even more AI at work - business spending set to double this year
item.description: IT departments are allocating more of their budgets, and more money overall, to artificial intelligence in 2025.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Using NATS with .NET Aspire
item.description: If you’re building a cloud-native application using a scalable cluster of microservices, you’re going to need a way for those services to communicate.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Legislators demand truth about OPM email server
item.description: Two members of the US House of Representatives want answers from the US Office of Personnel Management (OPM) over allegations that a “server of unknown nature” was used last month to “access sensitive government data without regard for crucial security and privacy protections.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life but they’re privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you’ve been.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nvidia's Spectrum-X Ethernet Networking Equipment Boosts Storage Read Bandwidth by 48%
item.description: Nvidia's Spectrum-X Ethernet networking equipment improves storage read bandwidth by 48% thanks to intelligence and adaptive routing, enhancing data processing for AI applications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Fast fashion, laptops and toys are likely to cost more due to US tariffs on Chinese imports
item.description: A sweeping new U.S. tariff on products made in China is expected to increase the prices American consumers pay for a wide array of products, from the ultra-cheap apparel sold on online shopping platforms to toys and electronic devices such as computers and cellphones.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Apple lashes out at iPhone porn app maker and the EU rules allowing its download
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple has chided a newly introduced pornography app available in the European Union, warning that the bloc’s digital rules opening the way for third-party app store downloads undermines consumer confidence in the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Google's rising holiday's season ad sales aren't enough to ease worries about AI letdown
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google's digital ad sales continued to grow at a healthy clip during the holiday season, but that wasn't enough to offset investors' worries about whether its big bet on artificial intelligence will be lucrative as once envisioned.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: China to investigate Google amid tensions with US
item.description: China said Tuesday it is investigating Google for potential antitrust violations after the Trump administration levied new tariffs on Beijing.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Google makes its appeal to overturn jury verdict branding the Play Store as illegal monopoly
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Google went to appeals court Monday in an attempt to convince a three-judge panel to overturn a jury's verdict declaring its app store for Android smartphones as an illegal monopoly and block the penalties imposed by a federal judge to stop the misbehavior.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: CISA hires former DHS CIO into top cyber position
item.description: Karen Evans is returning to the Department of Homeland Security in a top cyber job as DHS responds to major hacks into U.S. critical infrastructure. The post CISA hires former DHS CIO into top cyber position first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates explores the making of his internal operating system in new memoir
item.description: As he prepares to turn 70 later this year, Microsoft founder Bill Gates' new memoir explores how his childhood quirks, upbringing, friendships and experiences coalesced into shaping his internal operating system.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bill Gates shares his thoughts on vaccine backlash, Intel's woes and Google's antitrust battle
item.description: Although Microsoft founder Bill Gates sat down for an interview with The Associated Press to discuss his new memoir, “Source Code: My Beginnings,” he also shared his views on a variety of other topics, including vaccine conspiracy views about him, his thoughts on the struggles of longtime computer c
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: SoftBank and OpenAI set up joint company to push artificial intelligence services
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology giant SoftBank Group and OpenAI stepped up their AI partnership Monday, setting up a 50-50 held company called SB OpenAI Japan.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Review: Solver can code that for you
item.description: In the area of programming automation, sometimes it’s better to fix a software problem by first analyzing the whole repository rather than guessing about what files are relevant to the problem.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Texas governor orders ban on DeepSeek, RedNote for government devices
item.description: AUSTIN, Texas (AP) — Texas Republican Gov. Greg Abbott issued a ban on Chinese artificial intelligence company DeepSeek for government-issued devices, becoming the first state to restrict the popular chatbot in such a manner.
item.dc:creator: NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US cybersecurity agency's future role in elections remains murky under the Trump administration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nation’s cybersecurity agency has played a critical role in helping states shore up the defenses of their voting systems, but its election mission appears uncertain amid sustained criticism from Republicans and key figures in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: How federal land could generate lots of electricity
item.description: "We estimate 10 to 20 million homes could be served from the electricity that are produced from that renewable energy," said Trieu Mai. The post How federal land could generate lots of electricity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Why artificial intelligence could cause the lights to dim
item.description: "Data centers is one of the new industrial sectors that we've been looking at because it's part of the emerging economy and new technologies," said Arman Shehab The post Why artificial intelligence could cause the lights to dim first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Amazon sues state agency to block release of company records to Bezos-owned Washington Post
item.description: Amazon is suing a Washington state agency to prevent the release of some company materials to The Washington Post, the newspaper which is owned by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5
